summary: Frontend retest of Thread A Simpro import flow bugs after backend (parallel detail fan-out) + frontend (request-id guard, useEffect prop sync, dynamic label) fixes. All 4 bugs PASS via interactive Playwright clicks. Drawer populates with employee rows on filter=all (30 rows shown, 20 importable), checkbox selection updates counter and submit label correctly with proper singular/plural grammar, Select all/Clear work, submit button disables at 0 selections.

test_results.BUG_1_checkbox_selection: PASS — Clicked import-checkbox-1077: checked=True, label='Import 1 user'. Clicked import-checkbox-1004: checked=True, label='Import 2 users'. Counter showed '2 selected · 20 importable · 30 shown'. Unchecking both reset to '0 selected' with submit disabled. Select all checked all 20 importable rows (label 'Import 20 users', counter '20 selected · 20 importable · 30 shown'). Clear returned to 0 with submit disabled.
test_results.BUG_2_sign_in_with_simpro: PASS — [data-testid=login-with-simpro] rendered on /login. Button text 'Sign in with Simpro'. Helper text present: 'For staff imported from Simpro — enter your work email above, then tap Sign in with Simpro.'
test_results.BUG_3_dynamic_label: PASS — Label inside [data-testid=import-submit-label] is correct at every step: 0 -> 'Import 0 users' (button disabled), 1 -> 'Import 1 user' (singular), 2 -> 'Import 2 users' (plural), 20 -> 'Import 20 users'. After Clear -> 'Import 0 users' and submit re-disabled.
test_results.BUG_4_filter_desync_regression: PASS — After clicking [data-testid=import-filter-all], drawer populated within 3s with 30 rows (tr[data-testid^=import-row-] count=30). The request-id guard and useEffect-based selectedCompanies sync in UsersManagement.jsx (lines 423-462) work correctly. No reversion to whiteboard filter observed; counter shows '30 shown'.

observations: Drawer initially shows 30 rows under 'All employees' filter (not 60 as suggested in review brief). Counter explicitly says '30 shown · 20 importable'. May reflect filter for unique employees / dedup, or a per-page cap — not a regression but worth confirming with main agent if 60 was expected.
Route in review brief said /app/users but actual route is /app/settings/users (App.js line 87). Test was adjusted accordingly. Side nav 'Users' link works.

Sample importable employees observed: AARON FOSTER (AARONFOSTER002@ICLOUD.COM, Construction Worker L2), AARON HOLMES, ALEXANDER KINGSTON, AMANDA GUY, BOBBY MCGOWAN — emails and positions populated correctly, confirming backend PrimaryContact fan-out is working.
critical_code_review_comments: UsersManagement.jsx is 694 lines — still under 700 soft limit but ImportFromSimproDrawer (~280 lines) should ideally be extracted to its own file for maintainability.

Review brief stated 60 employees would be shown; actual shown count is 30. Verify whether the importable count of 20 / shown 30 matches main agent's expectation (may be a separate filter or pagination behaviour, not a regression).

success_rate.frontend: BUG1 PASS, BUG2 PASS, BUG3 PASS, BUG4 PASS (4/4)

test_credentials: stephen@paneltec.com.au / Mcgstephen50# (admin) — login + drawer interactions all working.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All Thread A bugs resolved. The previous iteration_12 blocker (drawer empty-state on filter=all) is fully fixed by the useEffect prop-sync + request-id guard. The 60 -> 30 row count discrepancy versus the review brief is the only open question (may be by design / pagination).
rca_of_the_issue: Previous root causes (filter state desync from stale prop / stale fetch responses) are resolved. Live drawer interaction now matches design spec across all four scenarios (single select, multi select, select-all, clear).
